summary: Body copy ≥16px with ≥150% line height; increase line height for light-on-dark text; use type scale for hierarchy.
Test colors for WCAG AA contrast minimums, avoid color-only meaning, ensure all text is legible per WCAG standards (wcag.com).
Form labels always visible; no placeholder-as-label or disappearing labels; click/tap targets must be at least 48px.
Use quick, non-disruptive transitions; avoid excessive motion; provide pause controls; support phone, tablet, desktop; no autoplay motion >5s without static fallback.
Animated GIFs in [GENERAL email] must convey the key message in frame 1 because Outlook displays only the first frame.
Provide descriptive alt text for all images and transcripts for all video/audio content to ensure accessibility.
Confirm audience in every brief before writing patient (DTC) materials per voice_tone guide.
Use "someone/people with dermatomyositis" in patient materials; "patients" is acceptable in HCP materials.
The patient brochure is the master reference for visuals, messaging, and tone across all LISRAYA materials.
Each section is a 600px wide self-contained <table> block with 32px top/bottom padding, declared background color, and no external margins; sections stack directly.
Section metadata must declare background color, DM status, footnote symbols used (†, ‡ assigned at assembly), and CTA presence.
Footnote symbols superscript, assigned in appearance order at assembly; sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., {{fn1}}) for multiple claims, not hard-coded daggers.
Locked header, footer, ISI inserted by core team; adjacent sections cannot have decorative elements visually dependent on those locked components (e.g., waves continuing into footer).
Dark-background sections must not be adjacent; assembler ensures this using declared backgrounds.
Use HEX colors, px sizing, inline CSS only, and duplicate bgcolor attributes for Outlook email compatibility.
LISRAYA palette colors ordered by dominance: Primary (Brand Blue, Sunshine, Gold) > Secondary (Sky Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy) > Tertiary (Coral, Light Blue, Golden Sand, Graphite, White).
Apply palette in primary > secondary > tertiary ratio: blues and golds dominate; tertiary used for accents and surfaces.
Headlines use Brand Blue or White on dark/gradient; body text uses Graphite #212121 on light, White on dark with increased line height; backgrounds follow semantic tokens.
In charts, use Brand Blue + Sky Blue as dominant series colors; Sunshine/Gold only for key figure emphasis; gray for placebo elements.
Sunshine/Gold only emphasizes key chart figures; never for general chart series colors.
Callout surfaces: Light Blue 70% tint for data; Golden Sand 30% tint for text-heavy callouts.
Use Coral sparingly as tertiary accent only; never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data; avoid in email unless specified by brief.
Links use Brand Blue #00529b underlined on light backgrounds, white underlined on dark backgrounds; background variants follow semantic tokens.
Logo icon uses 70% transparent Pantone 7548 C (#ffc60a) in all uses, including logos and mark patterns/graphics.
Use only palette or specified tints: campaign headline letters ≈40%, Light Blue callout box 70%, Golden Sand callout box 30%, waves top layer 70% opacity.
Campaign headline 'D' and 'M' letters use ~40% tint of headline color (Gold or Brand Blue), per campaign_headline.letter.tint.
Light Blue callout uses 70% tint opacity; Golden Sand callout uses 30% tint opacity.
Wave top layers must be rendered at 70% opacity (waves.top_layer.opacity).

Use only approved gradients: Brand Blue→Dark Navy, Sky Blue→Deep Blue, Sunshine→75% Gold, Golden Sand→White→Light Blue, Golden Sand→75% Gold→Sunshine, Masthead Sunshine→light gold; for backgrounds, wave graphic, LISRAYA logo.
Masthead gradient (Sunshine to light gold, tok_lisraya_masthead_gradient) is used with LISRAYA logo mark positioned at gradient's lightest end for contrast.
In masthead/banner, gradients run horizontally dark/saturated to light left to right (tok_lisraya_gradient_angle_horizontal).
Ensure every text/background pair meets WCAG AA contrast: minimum 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text (≥24px or 19px bold) using webaim.com or similar.
Use Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy/Graphite text on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White text on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy; avoid Gold/Sunshine text on White.
Avoid using color alone for meaning; combine with icons, depth, clear text, or visual feedback for accessibility.

Gradient Callout CTA: full-width 600px banner with centered stacked headline/button over orange/yellow gradient PNG bg (ast_lisraya_gradient_callout_bg) cover no-repeat center; fallback Sunshine #faa31b; padding 48px 25px 56px 25px.
Headline top-third, centered, Brand Blue (#00529b), Nunito Sans 800 weight, ~28px, single line.
cta button centered below headline, 248x64px, Deep Blue (#003D74) fill, white Nunito Sans 800 18px label, Gold (#FFC20E) 24px arrow '›' after two nbsp.
CTA buttons must always have the asymmetric border-radius 49.52px 1.76px 40px 1.76px exactly, without simplification, swapping, or rounding decimals.
Use approved hosted gradient PNG ast_lisraya_gradient_callout_bg as background; skip image only in rare cases, keep background-color #faa31b and other styles unchanged.

Icon callout box is Light Blue #E6F0F9 single-accent-shape with Brand Blue #00529b Nunito Sans 800 ~21px/28px left/vert-center headline; White #fff rounded-square icon badge with Brand Blue stroke, 40px 8px 40px 8px border radius, badge 20px 4px 20px 4px, consistent corners, badge has subtle drop shadow rgba(1,30,69,0.18).

Icon badge (80px wide) straddles box left edge (50% overlap), box inset 40px left with padding-left 64px, badge vertically centered top:50% margin-top:-40px; keep inset=W/2, padding=W/2+24px, margin-top=−H/2 for other sizes.

The 50/50 badge uses position:absolute honored by most clients except Outlook, which drops badge top-left; to ensure pixel-perfect fidelity, pre-composite badge as PNG or bake into background, else accept Outlook degrade with live elements and keep Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow unchanged.

Email body fixed at 600px width, centered; sections must be exactly 600px wide edge-to-edge; below 480px breakpoint, multi-column layouts collapse to single column; 12-column internal grid with practical 1-, 2-col (50/50), or 40/60 image/text splits.
All sections have 24px side padding; full-bleed backgrounds may extend to 600px but text preserves 24px insets for seamless assembly.
Use 8px base for vertical spacing; sections have 32px top/bottom padding, zero external margins; heading-body 16px, paragraph-paragraph 16px, body-CTA 24px, image-caption 8px.
Primary CTA: #00529b bg, #ffffff text, Nunito Sans Bold 16px, padding 14x32px, min 48px height, 24px radius pill; Secondary: white bg, 2px #00529b border, #00529b text same metrics.
One primary CTA allowed per section maximum.
Wave graphics use Sunshine/Gold or Sky Blue/Deep Blue colorways, in long/short forms, with a 70% opacity top wave layer matching specified translucency and approved colors.
Waves can be used alone or over photography/gradients to support layout, frame content, or add depth; only approved colors allowed.
Use masthead gradient (Sunshine to light gold) with logo mark at lightest end as the approved copy-holding device.
Photography features warm, realistic patient lifestyle images of simple activities (walking dog, beach, BBQ) showing returning to everyday life and self.
Images use Accent shape with asymmetric rounded corners per §2.5, avoiding plain rectangles or circles.
Waves and gradients can overlay photography in imagery.
Every image must include descriptive alt text to meet brand accessibility standards.
Brand Accent Shape: rectangle with diagonal opposite corner radii—large radius (print: 1.25", 0.5", or 0.4375"; email px: 120, 48, 42px) plus 0.0625"/6px on other corners.
Use consistent diagonal accent-shape radii orientation in emails; default large radius at top-left and bottom-right across all sections.
Charts and tables use a uniform 12px rounded corner radius on all four corners, not the asymmetric accent shape.
Charts use containers with uniform rounded corners, Light Blue (#E6F0F9) panel fill, and Brand Blue (#00529b) rounded header bars for clean, scannable layout.
Use Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) for series, Sunshine (#faa31b) and Gold (#ffc60a) for key emphasis, neutral gray for placebo/comparator.
Bars and chart icons must have rounded tops for a rounded, approachable shape.
Chart headlines use Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (email), ALL CAPS, Brand Blue color, with a thin Gold rule beneath.
Statistical callouts: large, bold % figure as hero, supporting copy lighter weight beneath.
Chart footnotes use smaller font size than primary data, distinctly separated for clear differentiation.
Icons use minimal single-color line illustrations with consistent stroke weight, rounded caps, Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds; new icons must match this style.
Icon colors and backgrounds must use only colors from the brand palette.
Iconography minimum render size 32×32px in email for scale and contrast to support low-vision audiences (icon.min_render_size).
Use generous white space, avoid clutter; limit focal points per section, employ contrast to highlight the single most important element.
Break text walls with imagery and icons to "show, don't tell" in layout.
Alternate My Compass webpage section backgrounds among White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, or gradient to avoid identical adjacent color blocks merging visually.
Designers must declare section background colors in metadata to prevent adjacent sections from having identical backgrounds during assembly.
Use bold headlines, simple benefit statements, and a patient quote as the canonical content block, following the patient brochure template for all LISRAYA materials.
Always write LISRAYA in ALL CAPS in all materials, superseding Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule.
Include ® only on first/most prominent external mention of brand with generic lowercase in parentheses; e.g., LISRAYA® (brepocitinib); no repeats.
Parent company Priovant Therapeutics, Inc. sign-off only in locked footer, never add in page sections.
Headlines use Agenda (print) or bold Nunito Sans Title Case (digital/email); body/footers use Nunito Sans; fallback Aptos for all; Agenda live text only in digital campaign headlines.
Email CSS font stack: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; load Nunito Sans via @font-face/Google Fonts if client supports, fallback for Outlook.
Email typography: H1 28/36 Nunito Sans ExtraBold/Bold; H2 22/30 Bold; H3 18/26 Bold/SemiBold; Body 16/24 Regular; Secondary 14/21 Regular sparingly; Footnote 12/18 smaller and separated.
Typography: Body copy minimum 16px (brand mandate), never below 12px running text; ISI sizing controlled by locked component.
Body copy line height minimum 150%; increase to 160% for light text on dark backgrounds; body.line_height token adjusts by background group.
Use distinct type scale and hierarchy; never use adjacent text blocks with same size and weight for different hierarchy levels.
Headlines and headings use title case: capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, prepositions.
Body copy uses sentence case typography.
Campaign headline "YOU DESERVE MORE" must always be in ALL CAPS per campaign headline lockup governance.
Chart/infographic headlines use ALL CAPS in Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (email fallback).
No ALL CAPS in body copy, buttons, subheads except campaign headlines and chart/infographic headlines.

Use bold sparingly for hierarchy: headings, one key phrase per paragraph, callout text/numbers per §1.7, first focal brand name per section; avoid full paragraphs, fillers, footnotes, over 15% body text.
Never combine bold, ALL CAPS, and color emphasis simultaneously on the same string, except in chart headlines and campaign headline lockup; max two emphasis devices per element.
No underlining except hyperlinks; italics reserved for study names, notes ('Note:'), and citations, not for emphasis in patient copy.
After first mention as "dermatomyositis (DM)", use "DM" in running copy to avoid repetition.
Do not use 'DM' in headlines, headings, or subheads; always spell out 'dermatomyositis' for clarity, accessibility, and SEO.
[GENERAL] Each standalone section must introduce 'dermatomyositis (DM)' upon first mention unless assembly brief confirms prior abbreviation use.
Use "someone/people with dermatomyositis" in patient materials (not "patients"); "patients" only in HCP materials; use "care team" (not "staff").
Spell out numbers 0–9; use numerals for 10+; always use numerals and % sign for percentages, ages, ratios.
Use empathetic, supportive tone avoiding negative/fear language when citing clinical data; e.g., highlight positive outcomes without oversimplifying.
Use Oxford/serial comma in lists of three or more items.
'YOU DESERVE MORE' headline: Agenda font (email fallback Nunito Sans ExtraBold), ALL CAPS, brand Gold/Sunshine or Blue colors; 'D' and 'M' at 40% tint; hero stacks three lines, left-aligned.
copy_editorial: Campaign lockup copy must be exactly "YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.
Use three approved callout styles (Main, Secondary/data, Text-heavy); never have two identical styles competing on one screen or section.
Main callout text: Bold Brand Blue, centered; Gold horizontal rules above/below, 2px thick, 60–100% width of text block, 16px padding.
Secondary/data callout: Light Blue 70% tint box, Bold Brand Blue text, optional one-line icon, 20px padding, corner radius per accent-shape §2.5.
Text-heavy callout uses 30% Golden Sand box, Bold Brand Blue headline, Bold Sky Blue subheads, Regular Graphite body, 24px padding, two-column collapses on mobile.
Campaign headline lockup must read exactly: "YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.

copy_editorial: Use lead-in "LISRAYA is a once-daily FDA-approved pill that blocks the specific inflammatory signals that drive dermatomyositis, so you can:" followed by exact bullets: Increase your muscle strength; Improve your skin symptoms; Rely less on steroids; Do more of the activities you love.
Mechanism-of-action copy must use the exact approved primary or alternate LISRAYA statements verbatim without alterations.
Administration statement must read verbatim: "LISRAYA is a once-daily pill—you can take it at home or anywhere you like, with no shots or infusions required.
(cta): Use exact CTA text: "Talk to your doctor about LISRAYA, the DM treatment you may be looking for.
Patient support program named "My Compass Support" offering Personalized Communication via PAL (text, phone, email, in person) and Authorization Support.
LISRAYA's voice: Confident (clear first-of-its-kind), Warm (genuine empathy), Clear (plain, respectful language), tone flexes by context but core remains consistent.
Clinical claims need footnote markers (†) with clearly separated, smaller footnote text per §1.2.
Patient quotes: attribute as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient` with italic/distinguished style plus warm lifestyle imagery.
Priovant logo, copyright, ® line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, ISI reside only in locked header/footer; never reproduce in content sections.
Use brand colors in fixed hierarchy: Deep Blue (#00529B) dominant, Amber Orange (#FAA31B) secondary, Golden Yellow (#FFC60A) tertiary, in primary > secondary > tertiary order.
Use Agenda for all headlines/headers; Nunito Sans for all print body copy.
Internal banner CTAs use 180x59 asymmetric rounded SVG shape with viewBox 0 0 180 59, filled with gradient #FFDF55 to #FAA21B.
